license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.8jfhujn
title: Limited storage of spittlebugs or green leafhoppers (under one week)
author: Niels Appelman

steps_raw.instruction: Cover the bottom of a PET container (e.g. https://www.world-of-bottles.co.uk/Glass-bottles/250ml-white-PET-jar-Bella-Mia-white.html) with moisturised single use towels (wetten with tapwater and squeeze to remove excess water) and provide airholes in container with an (injection) needle.
Invert the tube in which insects are temporarily stored and allow insects to climb up
While holding the tube above the new storage container, uncrew its cap and forcefully tap the back of the tube such that the insects drop into their new storage container.
Swiftly close the new storage container. Note: this step must be performed as quickly as possible, as it is crucial to minimize the time insects get to recover and escape.
Move insects into experimental setups as soon as possible, preferably within 2 days. Survival rate is highly variable. Upto 7 days of survival was achieved.
